interoperability.notes: Consume this payload instead of scraping ad hoc markdown or chat transcripts.

anti_stay_posture: 1. Separate agreements, separate forums — MNDA and SDA are independent contracts with independent dispute resolution. SDA arbitration cannot override MNDA exclusive court clause.
2. Non-signatory Tyler MacPherson cannot be compelled to arbitrate — personal liability claims must proceed in court.
3. MNDA Section 6 'irrespective of legal theory' anti-avoidance language prevents recharacterization of MNDA claims as SDA claims.
strategy_principles: Two-front architecture: Nigeria = battlefield (Dayo, SDA claims, agreed costs), Alberta = artillery (MNDA claims, $5K budget, pressure device).
Alberta filing exists to make settlement cheaper than resistance — not to be litigated to trial.
Every dollar NRG Bloom spends on Alberta should cost TON ten dollars to respond to.

latest_round.assessment: This is not yet a fully verified revenue record, but it is a strong operations-and-control indicator: restored site power, active mining, then loss of NRG-side pool visibility consistent with takeover of machine configuration.
latest_round.next_step: Preserve and verify evidence that TON began mining on 2026-03-06 after terminating NRG Bloom.

live_next_actions: Preserve and verify evidence that TON began mining on 2026-03-06 after terminating NRG Bloom.
Decide whether to prepare a protective Alberta filing package now.
Update settlement tracker to reflect self-directed Canadian litigation posture and the reported mining commencement.
Convert core claims, chronology, and evidence map into filing-ready structure.
matter.live_notes: McCarthy Tetrault engagement is paused. Canadian path is now self-directed with AI support. The runtime should prefer this file over older March 2026 references that assume a live McCarthy call. User reports TON began mining on 2026-03-06 after terminating NRG Bloom beforehand; treat this as a major damages and leverage development pending evidence preservation and verification. User also reports that the NRG pool dashboard showed renewed mining activity on 2026-03-06 followed by loss of visibility hours later, consistent with restored power/connectivity and a likely post-restart switch of pool wallet settings. Eyewitness/local team reports say the transformer was commissioned on the same date.
matter.name: NRG Bloom Inc. v. TON Infrastructure Ltd.
red_lines: Walk-away floor: $500,000 USD — no settlement below this
No settlement without mutual release of all claims
No settlement without written acknowledgment of NRG Bloom's contribution (internal, non-public)
No settlement that waives Axxela-related claims without adequate compensation
No settlement that includes non-compete restricting NRG Bloom's Nigeria operations

ranked_actions.rationale: The Canadian track is still unresolved and the Axxela circumvention claim remains the main cross-border leverage point.
ranked_actions.settlement_pressure: A credible Alberta filing path makes it more expensive for TON to resist by preserving a second front tied to the Axxela opportunity.
ranked_actions.title: Decide and prepare the protective Alberta filing path

ranked_actions.rationale: At least one tracked litigation deadline is already past in the live matter state.
ranked_actions.settlement_pressure: A clean live record reduces TON's ability to hide behind delay, drift, or ambiguity in the dispute process.
ranked_actions.title: Refresh the dispute clock and document the live negotiation posture

ranked_actions.rationale: The services and expenditure theory is identified, but the damages schedule is still not tight enough.

ranked_actions.title: Quantify the unjust-enrichment and operations damages schedule

ranked_actions.rationale: The witness statement remains one of the fastest ways to harden a contested oral admission.

ranked_actions.title: Secure Julie's witness statement on the calculated-tactic admission

ranked_actions.rationale: The prior lawsuit pattern is useful leverage but the record is still incomplete in the live queue.
